summary:
The patient exhibits hallmark signs of phenylketonuria including blue eyes, blond hair, and fair skin along with a confirmed diagnosis via a positive Guthrie test. Option B stating that "white patches appear in hair" is false because such findings result from tryptophan deficiency—not PKU—and thus represents an inaccurate statement about the condition.
